author: marioevz
content:
## ACD-T Summary + EL Breakout (AI):

### Glamsterdam Devnet 6 Status

- Participation around 80%+ overall
- Minor recovery issues: Lodestar+Besu and Lodestar+Erigon nodes hung after fork
  - EL refused to update its head; Lodestar+Besu recovered after restart
  - Lodestar+Erigon left as-is for now, pending investigation
- Nimbus had an issue, now resolved
- Grandine: P99 block production delay traced to late event publishing, not a deeper issue

### Glamsterdam Devnet 7 Launch

- Releases: EL [v7.2.0](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/releases/tag/tests-glamsterdam-devnet%40v7.2.0), CL [v1.7.0-alpha.12](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/releases/tag/v1.7.0-alpha.12)
- Stefan planning to launch devnet 7 after the call or by Tuesday morning
- Launch config:
  - 500k exited validators, 1024 ETH balance, 0x02 withdrawal credentials
  - Manual deployment of the 8282 contract to simulate live network conditions
  - Gas limit: **300 million** (raised from Stefan’s initial 200M; agreed on the call)
  - Fuzzing transactions to start post-fork, mixed types
- Pre-launch issues already reported: peer scoring with Prism, P2P issue with Nimbus, Nethermind outbound peer connection failure
- Note raised: gas limit discussions should include CL teams, as CL is now a determining factor in slot restructuring

### EL Client Test Readiness (Glamsterdam 7)

- Nethermind and Erigon: passing most tests, working well in local Kurtosis networks
- Besu: ~40 failing tests on local branch, mostly a repricing issue; majority passing; push planned to test in Kurtosis
- Reth: passing all state and blockchain tests; changes under review, branch expected by Tuesday
- Grandine: no update; team member working on it not yet synced
- CL side: Lighthouse and Lodestar passing all tests; Grandine follow-up pending

### EIP-8037 Bug Fix (Breaking Change in v7.2.0)

- Bug: blocks could be constructed up to 8x expected max size
  - Root cause: data floor cost accounting applied only at transaction level, not block level
- Fix: floor cost now applied at block level; merged into specs and EL v7.2.0 release
- Impact: minor, changed 2-3 existing tests; one extra test added
- Clarification: not a DoS per-se, but violated the max block size assumptions from EIP-7623
- Clients need to update implementations to pass tests correctly

### EIP Cleanup Proposals for Devnet 8

- Maria proposed two non-consensus-breaking cleanups for Amsterdam EIPs:
  1. Rename regular gas in EIP-8037 to execution gas for forward compatibility with multi-dimensional gas
  2. Restructure log costs in EIP-2780: consolidate separate log transfer costs into base cost + transfer-with-value (values sum to same 21k total, just reorganized)
- Rationale: EIPs are the primary public-facing document for the fork; terminology should be clear before finalization
- General agreement: desirable, simplifies readability, restrict to cleanup only (no further consensus changes)
- Maria to draft PRs and share with devs; targeting next week at latest

### Next Steps

- **Launch **Glamsterdam** devnet 7** (Stefan)
  - Targeting post-call today or Tuesday morning; 300M gas limit, 500k exited validators, 8282 contract manual deploy.
- **Investigate Lodestar Eragon fork recovery hang**
  - Node left in hung state; EL refused to update head post-fork.
- **Draft EIP cleanup PRs for Amsterdam devnet 8** (Maria)
  - Rename execution gas variable and restructure log costs in EIP-2780; share with devs by next week.
- **Review and comment on Amsterdam upgrade mascot decision**
  - Link shared in chat; clients to flag any opposition.
